title: Calonema pachychilum
taxonomy: Calonema pachychilum is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Calonema is part of the subtribe Coelogyninae. The subtribe Coelogyninae is a subunit of the tribe Coelogyneae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Calonema pachychilum is a tropical plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Traditional medicine has utilized Calonema pachychilum for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds, infections, and inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Calonema pachychilum include alkaloids, such as calonemine and calonemin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antiproliferative activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Calonema pachychilum include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
